steps.title: Use the discriminant
steps.content:
A quadratic has no real solution when its discriminant is negative. For $-3x^2+bx-27=0$, use $a=-3$ and $c=-27$:
$$
b^2-4(-3)(-27)<0
$$

steps.title: Find the allowed values
steps.content:
Simplify the inequality:
$$
b^2-324<0
$$
$$
b^2<324
$$
So:
$$
-18<b<18
$$

steps.title: Pick the largest integer
steps.content: Since $b$ is a positive integer, the possible values are $1$ through $17$. At $b=18$, the discriminant would be $0$, giving one real solution instead of no real solution. The answer is 17.
